qa_pairs.question: What defines a general-purpose compute cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: A general-purpose compute cluster is engineered to support a diverse range of job types and comprises a mix of different nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What are the various types of nodes found within compute clusters, and what are their typical memory specifications?
qa_pairs.answer: Compute clusters typically include base nodes with approximately 4GB of memory per core, large-memory nodes providing more than 8GB of memory per core, and GPU nodes equipped with graphic processing units.

qa_pairs.question: Which of the listed general-purpose compute clusters are currently in production?
qa_pairs.answer: According to the provided list, the general-purpose compute clusters currently in production are Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual.
